ksa_text: Knowledge of privacy technologies and data protection tools, including encryption standards, anonymization and de-identification techniques, privacy-enhancing technologies, data minimization implementations, and monitoring tools for privacy compliance.

Transparency and provision of information to deployers

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA SP-K-010 (SP, Knowledge) does not clear the weak fit inclusion threshold of 0.52. Composite score 0.4253 is insufficient to establish relevance to performance metrics, KPI analysis, and operational reporting at IC-to-senior-IC level. Framework signals (nice=0.425, dcwf=0.407, ddat=0.392) do not demonstrate sufficient alignment with core analytics/BI work.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 exclude upheld. Score 0.4253 below threshold for weak fit domain; no rescuing analytics text signals. No internal inconsistencies identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does SP-domain KSA SP-K-010 (composite 0.4253) appear in published data analyst or BI role profiles?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: SP domain content (AI governance, security/privacy, risk management, compliance) is not a primary expectation in IC-to-senior-IC ANL roles per NIST CSF, ISO 27001; security competencies in IC ANL scope limited to data access controls awareness, not security architecture. Composite 0.4253 confirms weak signal. Exclusion consistent with published ANL frameworks.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 exclude upheld. External evidence confirms SP domain KSA at composite 0.4253 is not a standard requirement for Performance Analyst at IC-to-senior-IC level.

decision_rationale: Pipeline consensus: exclude. P2 exclude confirmed by P3 (internal) and P4 (external) adversarial agents. Zero overturns. Composite 0.4253, domain SP, scope weak fit. High confidence.
